frame_note: No scalar Mexican lifetime NPV. Model education × age-at-arrival × state × descendants. NAS <HS age-25 ≈ -$109k (2012$); Clemens capital-tax band flips sign.
staging_gaps: external/lifetime/nrc/nrc_1997_new_americans.pdf is an HTML landing page, not the 1997 report PDF — NRC 1997 numbers below relay via Orrenius (2017) citing NAS/NRC lineage.

external/lifetime/nber/storesletten_2003_fiscal_heterogeneity_w9489.pdf is mis-staged (NBER w9489 Blomström/Kokko FDI incentives, not Storesletten fiscal heterogeneity) — Storesletten (2000) parameters cited via Colas-Sachs (2024).

parameter_claims.population: Immigrant arriving at age 25, <HS education, CBO long-term budget outlook, public goods excluded
parameter_claims.notes: Individual-only column; immigrant less costly than native (-$251k) mainly via lower welfare uptake. Treating 'no public goods' as neutral hides large sensitivity.

parameter_claims.population: New immigrant resembling recent arrivals (past 5 years), immigrant + descendants, CBO scenario
parameter_claims.notes: Highlighted cell '259'; $173k individual + $85k descendants. CBO future budget assumptions drive magnitude — No Budget Adjustments scenario yields $77k total.
3% real discount; with 1% productivity growth effective rate ~2%. Retirement benefits discounted ~35 years later → NPV heavily front-loaded to working years.

parameter_claims.population: Second generation group, all government levels, 1994-2013 cross-section
parameter_claims.notes: 2013 total shortfall $279B (1st gen), $109B (2nd gen), $856B (3rd+). Distinct from lifetime NPV — easy to conflate in debate.

parameter_claims.population: Immigrants with less than high school education
parameter_claims.page_ref: secondary: orrenius pdftotext p.5 citing NRC 1997 via NAS Ch.7
parameter_claims.notes: Primary PDF not extractable (HTML stub). Orrenius: '<HS immigrants cost $89,000 more than they contribute in taxes over lifetimes.' Assumed fiscal sustainability in NRC descendants modeling.

parameter_claims.population: Immigrants with more than high school education
parameter_claims.notes: Relay only. NRC assumed policy would adjust to restore fiscal balance — descendants net positive partly by construction.

parameter_claims.population: Average recent immigrant <HS, individual only, capital-tax adjustment applied to NAS baseline
parameter_claims.page_ref: pdftotext p.1 (abstract); p.4 (NAS comparison); Table 3 p~25
parameter_claims.notes: Sign flip from NAS -$109k to ≥+$128k by adding omitted capital tax revenue (α=0.436, τ_K/τ_L from Saez-Zucman). Assumes fixed capital stock short-run + profit-maximizing firms.

parameter_claims.population: Average recent U.S. immigrant (all education levels)
parameter_claims.notes: Conservative capital-tax correction raises standard partial-equilibrium estimates by factor 3.2; education gradient flattens (BA/postgrad ratio 2.6 vs 4.5 unadjusted).
Used in equation (14) correction; robustness claimed for all modern labor-share values.

parameter_claims.population: Average low-skilled immigrant, preferred spec σ=2, fixed labor supply
parameter_claims.page_ref: pdftotext p.6; Table main results p~24 ($753)
parameter_claims.notes: Wage-incidence channel via CES production; 1% gov discount rate for NPV annex tables. HS dropouts stay net negative even after +$641–778 indirect.

parameter_claims.population: HS dropout immigrant, marginal cost = average cost public goods
parameter_claims.notes: With MC=0 (zero marginal public goods) direct = -$86/yr; total with indirect ≈ $744/yr. Public-goods allocation dominates sign.

parameter_claims.population: U.S. natives, standard generational accounting (immigrants folded into natives)
parameter_claims.notes: Revised immigrant-explicit GA finds 54% imbalance vs 72% GPS — attribution convention moves the needle.

parameter_claims.population: Future-generation males, 'responsibility' scenario, defense as pure public good
parameter_claims.notes: Eliminating immigration hurts future gens by $2,200 (M) / $1,700 (F) under defense-fixed assumption; reverses sign vs proportional gov purchases. Tiny vs $49,700 newborn male baseline imbalance.
Down from 72% (GPS) / 54% (Lee-Miller w/ old outlook). Improved baseline reduces immigration's marginal fiscal relief.

parameter_claims.unit: 2012 USD NPV (75-year, relay of NAS Table 8-12)
parameter_claims.population: Typical recent immigrant lacking HS diploma
parameter_claims.notes: Orrenius Table 2 row <HS = -109 to -117 depending on column; emphasizes education heterogeneity over population-average +$259k headline.

unnamed_assumptions_cross_cutting: Public goods treated as zero marginal cost (pure nonrival) vs average-cost allocation — swings NAS <HS from deeply negative to near-zero or positive.
Future budget path (CBO long-term vs no-adjustments) embeds optimistic/pessimistic benefit growth without sensitivity labels in headlines.
Age-25 arrival counterfactual excludes pre-arrival education costs for immigrants and parental finance for natives — framed as neutral lifecycle comparison.
Generational accounting counts taxes minus transfers, largely excluding education spending except occasional treatment — education cost pushed off balance sheet.
Partial-equilibrium fiscal accounting assumes zero short-run capital tax response (fixed K) — Clemens shows this is load-bearing for sign.
Immigrant vs native comparison holds education constant but not language, network, or emigration — presented as apples-to-apples fiscal test.
Discount rate (1–3% real) and 75-year horizon treated as standard rather than welfare-weighted choice.
Composition (education mix) vs level (immigrant count) — Auerbach/Lee-Miller: composition dominates, but policy debate still scalar.

generators.name: Capital-tax omission audit
generators.prompt: For each lifetime fiscal NPV study in the corpus: list every tax base counted on the revenue side. If corporate/capital income is absent, mechanically apply α/(1-α) × τ_K/τ_L × labor tax base (Clemens 2023 eq. 14) and re-rank education cells by sign. Flag any headline scalar that flips.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced Clemens (2023) capital-tax flip (-$109k→+$128k for <HS) AND Colas-Sachs critique that Storesletten (2000) omits capital-supply response — both hinge on the same omitted tax base before reading either paper's policy conclusion.
generators.negative_space: Standing frame treats 'taxes paid by immigrants' as labor+payroll cashflow only; excludes owner-side capital incidence entirely.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Fixed capital stock in short run ⇒ zero capital tax response
Partial equilibrium fiscal accounting = total fiscal effect
generators.theory_tested: If α×τ_K channel is material, annual federal proxy rows should correlate with labor tax base × education_bucket but NOT with raw transfer receipt rates alone.

generators.name: Public-goods allocation stress grid
generators.prompt: Build a 3×3 grid for every NPV table: {zero marginal, 0.25×AC, full AC} × {exclude defense, include defense, include all public goods}. Extract the cell each paper reports as 'the' estimate. Never cite a scalar without grid coordinates.
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged NAS Table 8-12/8-13 dual panels (public goods in/out) AND Orrenius marginal-cost sensitivity AND Colas-Sachs Table 3 (-$86/yr vs -$2,429/yr for HS dropouts) before treating -$109k as robust.
generators.negative_space: Debates anchor a single NPV number; papers bury allocation rules in table notes.
generators.theory_tested: DuckDB scenario_ledger should expose public_goods_scenario dimension; join to npv_education_benchmarks.adjustment column.

generators.name: Composition-vs-level decomposition
generators.prompt: For generational-accounting immigration papers: (1) simulate level change holding 1990 education mix fixed; (2) simulate education-mix shift holding count fixed; (3) report which moves fiscal imbalance >10× more than the other. Reject any study reporting only total immigrant NPV.
generators.retrodiction: Would have extracted Auerbach-Oreopoulos (2000) finding that composition beats level AND Lee-Miller (1998) $2.2k vs $49.7k scale result AND NAS contrast of recent (+$259k) vs all-immigrant (+$58k) pools.
generators.negative_space: Policy argument uses 'more immigrants' scalar; literature says education mix is first-order.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrant count policy is separable from skill selectivity
generators.theory_tested: education_bucket share shifts in ACS should predict NPV benchmark selection (recent vs stock) better than total foreign-born count.

generators.name: Unit harmonizer: annual incidence vs lifetime NPV
generators.prompt: Whenever a fiscal claim uses $/year (incidence, MVPF, annuitized direct), convert to 75-year NPV at stated discount AND label incomparable if indirect/general-equilibrium. Block synthesis until units match.
generators.retrodiction: Would have caught Colas-Sachs ~$753/yr indirect vs NAS -$109k lifetime as different objects AND Clemens factor-3.2 adjustment on same NAS table AND Orrenius mixing cross-section billions with NPV thousands.
generators.negative_space: Standing frame collapses 'fiscal effect' into one ledger line.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Annual wage-incidence externality commensurate with lifetime NPV
Cross-section annual shortfall ≈ lifetime immigrant cost
generators.theory_tested: Compare life.npv_education_benchmarks (USD NPV) to ctx annual federal proxy; correlation should break for Colas-Sachs rows tagged indirect_annual_usd.

generators.name: Immigrant attribution convention check
generators.prompt: In generational accounting papers: trace whether immigrants' taxes are folded into native cohorts or separate Q_{s,k} terms. Recompute headline imbalance under both conventions. Document which convention each citation uses.
generators.retrodiction: Would have found Lee-Miller 54% vs GPS 72% imbalance gap AND Green-Kotlikoff warning that fiscal labels are reference-dependent AND NAS Table 8-13 native/immigrant pairing.
generators.negative_space: Treats generational accounting as objective balance sheet.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrants attributed to native generational accounts
Equal percentage tax hikes on natives and immigrants is realistic closure rule
generators.theory_tested: Warehouse should tag npv_education_benchmarks.study with attribution_convention metadata; falsify if two studies at same education_bucket differ only by convention.

generators.name: Descendant ledger split
generators.prompt: For every lifetime NPV, force extraction of {individual, descendants, total} columns. Flag papers that report total only. Test whether descendant surplus drives positive headline for low-skilled arrivals.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced NAS $173k individual + $85k descendants (= $259k) AND Clemens -$109k individual vs +$326k with grandchildren AND NAS No-Budget scenario where descendants are -$15k while immigrant +$92k.
generators.negative_space: Headline 'immigrant fiscal impact' silently includes second/third generation without education×age path.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Descendant education upgrade is causal fiscal credit to arrival cohort
Intermarriage and emigration ignored in descendant projection
generators.theory_tested: Filter npv_education_benchmarks.includes_descendants; individual_npv should remain negative for <HS under NAS baseline while total may flip.

theories_tested.theory: Education bucket is sufficient statistic for lifetime NPV rank order
theories_tested.prediction: Within NAS/Clemens benchmarks, <HS < HS < some college < BA < postgrad monotonic in individual_npv_2012_usd at age 25
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT acs_education_bucket, individual_npv_2012_usd, study, adjustment FROM lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks WHERE age_at_arrival=25 AND includes_descendants=false ORDER BY individual_npv_2012_usd;
theories_tested.falsifier: Any pair inverted after holding adjustment constant (e.g., HS worse than <HS under same public_goods_scenario)

theories_tested.theory: Capital-tax adjustment flips sign only for lowest education buckets
theories_tested.prediction: Clemens capital_tax_adjustment rows positive for <HS; NAS baseline rows negative for <HS; ratio post/pre ≈ 3.2 for average bucket
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT study, acs_education_bucket, individual_npv_2012_usd FROM lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks WHERE acs_education_bucket='<HS' AND study IN ('NAS 2017','Clemens 2023');
theories_tested.falsifier: Clemens-adjusted NPV still negative for <HS in warehouse after re-seed from Table 3

theories_tested.theory: Annual federal proxy correlates with lifetime NPV rank across education buckets
theories_tested.prediction: ctx federal microsim annual net contribution by education_bucket should rank-order match life.npv_education_benchmarks individual NPV
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT f.education_bucket, e.weighted_adults AS acs_stock, AVG(f.federal_net_proxy_annual) AS annual_federal_net_proxy, b.individual_npv_2012_usd, b.study FROM context.acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals_2023 e JOIN fiscal.v_origin_federal_with_education_npv f ON e.education_bucket=f.education_bucket LEFT JOIN lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks b ON e.education_bucket=b.acs_education_bucket WHERE b.study='NAS 2017' GROUP BY f.education_bucket, e.weighted_adults, b.individual_npv_2012_usd, b.study ORDER BY f.education_bucket
theories_tested.falsifier: Annual proxy positive for <HS while NAS NPV negative — indicates unit/scope mismatch (supports G-LIF-A04)

theories_tested.theory: Indirect annual incidence does not rescue HS dropouts in Colas-Sachs framework
theories_tested.prediction: Colas-Sachs indirect_annual_usd (~750) + direct annuitized NPV still net negative for HS dropouts under MC=AC public goods
theories_tested.duckdb_test: SELECT * FROM lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks WHERE study='Colas-Sachs 2024' AND acs_education_bucket='<HS'; -- compare magnitude to NAS -109k; annual 750×40yr ≠ 109k without discount harmonization
theories_tested.falsifier: Harmonized NPV of indirect+$753/yr exceeds |direct| for dropouts under stated 1% discount

theories_tested.theory: Recent-arrival pool NPV exceeds stock-average NPV conditional on education mix
theories_tested.prediction: Weighted mean of npv by recent-arrival education shares > stock shares, controlling for adjustment
theories_tested.duckdb_test: WITH tot AS (SELECT SUM(weighted_adults) s FROM context.acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals_2023) SELECT b.study, SUM((e.weighted_adults / (SELECT s FROM tot)) * b.individual_npv_2012_usd) AS weighted_npv FROM context.acs_foreign_born_education_bucket_totals_2023 e JOIN lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks b ON e.education_bucket=b.acs_education_bucket WHERE b.study='NAS 2017' GROUP BY b.study
theories_tested.falsifier: Stock and recent weights produce same weighted NPV within 5% — contradicts NAS $259k vs $58k narrative

theories_tested.theory: No single origin_label scalar maps to lifetime NPV
theories_tested.prediction: Mexican or any origin share vector produces wide NPV range when joined to education_bucket benchmarks — variance dominates mean
theories_tested.duckdb_test: WITH comp AS (SELECT origin_label, education_bucket, SUM(weighted_adults) AS wa FROM context.acs_origin_household_federal_microsim_2023 GROUP BY origin_label, education_bucket) SELECT m.origin_label, m.education_bucket, m.wa / SUM(m.wa) OVER (PARTITION BY m.origin_label) AS share, b.individual_npv_2012_usd FROM comp m JOIN lifetime.npv_education_benchmarks b ON m.education_bucket=b.acs_education_bucket WHERE b.study='NAS 2017' ORDER BY m.origin_label, m.education_bucket
theories_tested.falsifier: One origin's education mix maps to a stable scalar cited in advocacy without bucket decomposition
